page: Amazon Web Services x8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2159.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2814.0. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also register strong results at 34.54 GB/sec and 14.38 GB/sec respectively. However, multi-core scaling presents a tradeoff; the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a similar divergence, with large-block write operations performing at an average level of 29.22 GB/sec, while read-only memory bandwidth is weak at 38.67 GB/sec for large blocks.

The hardware profile of the x8i.xlarge offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without intensive parallel processing demands. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for specialized vector and matrix operations. The instance is suited for memory-bound applications such as Redis caching, database operations, text processing, and light language model inference, though it is constrained for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core but lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, including top-tier text processing and strong single-threaded compression, though multi-core CPU performance and read-only memory bandwidth are weak. Supported by AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive, single-thread-heavy workloads. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, suited for database and memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high DDR5 memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16 GB per vCPU
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing capabilities
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via Intel AMX and AVX-512
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps
Suited for Redis caching, database operations, and text processing
